effort_complexity.rationale: One bounded TUI journey across eight files; concurrency identity and terminal layout arithmetic make it a small but subtle puzzle.

violations.description: AC-04's root-model scrolling journey does not exercise the full bounded domains it claims to prove: it stops at release cursor 18 of 25 and board cursor 12 of 18, so a cap after either tested position would still pass while violating the requirement that every loaded release and ordered slice is reachable.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent operator can navigate many releases and slices inside a terminal-height-bounded TUI that loads only the recent catalog depth they need.

positive_scenarios: A project with 25 releases opens with only the newest 10 materialised, then exposes 20 after one explicit older request.
A short terminal keeps both pane cursors reachable, and shrinking or growing it preserves selected IDs.
Right/Left and Enter/Esc navigate the same release-list and board focus states.
negative_scenarios: A hidden slice of a complete catalog cannot masquerade as bounded work; excluded board/status objects are not materialised.
A stale smaller-depth refresh cannot erase older rows after the operator requests a larger depth.
Help, errors, borders, or tiny heights cannot push the final rendered frame beyond a positive reported terminal height.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Bounding both discovery work and rendered rows turns the ref-aware TUI into a fast, reachable navigation surface even as a repository accumulates releases and slices.

design_decisions.choice: Prime 10 newest releases and let lowercase o grow the requested depth by 10.
options: five releases with automatic end-of-list loading
ten releases with explicit lowercase o loading
design_decisions.human_decision: The repository owner delegated a five-or-ten batch and required older access; the planner selected the explicit ten-record option from the current keybinding architecture.
design_decisions.rationale: It is bounded, predictable, discoverable in help, and does not collide because o is interpreted by focus state.

design_decisions.choice: Use measured terminal chrome and independent cursor-relative row windows for the two panes.
options: measure rendered chrome and clamp derived heights
render everything and truncate the final string only
design_decisions.human_decision: The repository owner required terminal-height reflow and scroll regions for both releases and board slices.
design_decisions.rationale: Measured allocation keeps selection-aware components in control and remains correct when help or error rows change.

design_decisions.choice: Add Right/Left as aliases while preserving Enter/Esc and one shared transition per direction.
design_decisions.human_decision: The repository owner explicitly requested Right into the board and Left back to releases as well as Esc.
design_decisions.rationale: Spatial navigation becomes natural without breaking the existing keyboard contract.
